title: A bill to rescind the unobligated balances of amounts appropriated for Internal Revenue Service enhancements and use such funding for an External Revenue Service.
plain_summary: This bill takes back money that was set aside to improve the Internal Revenue Service but hasn't been spent yet. The money was supposed to help the IRS collect taxes better, provide better customer service, update computer systems, and research ways to make filing taxes easier. The bill also takes back unused money that was meant for other tax-related offices that work with the IRS. Instead of using this money for the IRS, Congress thinks it should be used to create a brand new agency called the External Revenue Service.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.

sections.title: Section 1  Rescission of Enhanced Internal Revenue Service Resources
sections.text: Part A - Taking Back Money: Congress is taking back all money that was given to improve the Internal Revenue Service (the government agency that collects taxes) but has not been spent yet. This money came from a law called the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022. Part B - What Congress Thinks Should Happen: Congress believes that the same amount of money that was taken back should be given to create and run a new agency called the External Revenue Service.
